CONFIDENTIAL 
Robin B. Wakshull 
Deputy District Attorney 
County of Santa Clara 
270 Grant Avenue 
Palo Alto, CA 
94306 
Dear Ms. Wakshull: 
December 7, 1984 
Re: 
Your Telephone Request for 
Analysis; Our No. A-84-300 
Pursuant to your telephone request, we have reviewed the 
newspaper article regarding the actions of Morgan Hill 
Councilmember William H. Brown. 
It is our opinion that under 
the current wording of our regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18700, such conduct does not violate the provisions of the 
Political Reform Act. 
However, if the proposed revisions to 
this regulation are adopted by the Commission at its January 
meeting, such conduct would be prohibited in the future. 
I have 
previously forwarded to you a copy of those proposed 
regulations, but I enclose another with this letter and, again, 
invite your comments or those of your agency. 
REL:plh 
Enclosures

Judy Davis

By Mike Castro 
Bee Staff Writer 
Four West Sacramento communi-
ty leaders are protesting the city's 
decision to hire an outside firm to 
oversee the construction of a 
$19 million water project. 
In a 
meeting tonight, the 
i) City Council is set to award a con-
tract worth about $650,000 to an as 
yet undisclosed firm. 
However, leaders of a years-long 
fight to convert West Sacramento's 
system from well water to surface 
water taken from the Sacramento 
River met Monday with City Manag-
er Gene Roll to protest the hirIng of 
an outside firm.

However, if the proposed revisions to this regulation are adopted by the Commission at its January meeting, such conduct would be prohibited in the future.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the conduct of Morgan Hill Councilmember William H. Brown, as described in a newspaper article, violates the Political Reform Act under the current wording of California Administrative Code Section 18700.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the councilmember's conduct does not currently violate the Political Reform Act under existing regulations, but would be prohibited if proposed regulatory revisions are adopted at the January Commission meeting.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Letter contains clear conclusion but no formal QUESTION section header; question inferred from context. Document contains extraneous newspaper article (Sacramento Bee, May 13, 1987) at end that appears to be OCR artifact or misfiled material unrelated to the FPPC opinion. OCR quality is fair with some scanning artifacts.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the conduct of Morgan Hill Councilmember William H. Brown, as described in a newspaper article, violates the Political Reform Act under the current wording of California Administrative Code Section 18700.

CONCLUSION: It is our opinion that under the current wording of our regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700, such conduct does not violate the provisions of the Political Reform Act. However, if the proposed revisions to this regulation are adopted by the Commission at its January meeting, such conduct would be prohibited in the future.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a telephone request from the Santa Clara County Deputy District Attorney regarding Morgan Hill Councilmember William H. Brown's conduct, concluding it does not violate current law but would violate proposed regulatory amendments under consideration.
